_comment: Parity catch-rate corpus — codex vs gpt-oss-120b on OUR OWN code. Each entry reintroduces a REAL defect codex caught during the 16-round hardening of the openrouter provider, by a precise anchor→replacement edit in source. The harness reapplies each break, runs `rea review` (codex authoritative + gpt-oss shadow via `--provider both`), records whether EACH reviewer flags it, then restores via `git checkout`. `severity` is the class codex assigned. A team extends this file: add an entry with a stable `anchor` (must occur exactly once in `file`) and a `replacement` that reintroduces the bug. Stale anchors are reported, not silently skipped.

cases.replacement: return modelVerdict; // PARITY-BREAK(verdict-laundering): trust the model's self-verdict over its findings
cases.description: Adapter trusts the model's self-verdict, so {verdict:'pass', findings:[P1]} writes a passing coverage record. A reviewer should flag that the model's verdict is not reconciled against finding severities.

A failed `git diff` collapses to an empty string, so a partial/empty patch is reviewed and a PASS is recorded claiming HEAD was reviewed. A reviewer should flag the silent-empty-on-failure as false coverage.

cases.anchor:
const trailingDirStar = glob.endsWith('/**');
  if (trailingDirStar) glob = glob.slice(0, -'/**'.length);
cases.replacement:
const trailingDirStar = false; // PARITY-BREAK(evidentiary-root-bypass): dir/** no longer matches the bare root
  if (glob.endsWith('/**')) glob = glob.slice(0, -'/**'.length);
cases.description: `strawn-legal/**` stops matching the bare `strawn-legal` directory, so a symlink whose realpath is the evidentiary root slips the guard and sends protected content off-machine. A reviewer should flag the dir-root glob gap.
served_by is sanitized BEFORE the backend-pin check, so a forged control-char id launders into a clean value that passes the pin. A reviewer should flag that sanitization defeats pin verification.

Dropping --no-ext-diff/--no-textconv lets a repo-configured GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF/textconv driver emit arbitrary bytes that go off-machine past the path-guard. A reviewer should flag that the diff content is not the raw patch.

cases.replacement: return false; // PARITY-BREAK(doctor-missing-key-warn): never treat a missing key as fatal
cases.description: doctor never reports a missing key as fatal even when openrouter is the active enforced lane (where `rea review` exits 2). A reviewer should flag the health check under-reporting a broken configuration.
